titles.title: SpinVibe: Simulating spin-phonon coupling for molecular qubits

institution.institution_name: Department of Materials Science and Engineering, Northwestern University, United States
Department of Materials Science and Engineering, Northwestern University, United States

citation.article_title: Enhancing spin coherence in optically addressable molecular qubits through host-matrix control
citation.author: Bayliss
citation.unstructured_citation: Bayliss, S. L., Deb, P., Laorenza, D. W., Onizhuk, M., Galli, G., Freedman, D. E., & Awschalom, D. D. (2022). Enhancing spin coherence in optically addressable molecular qubits through host-matrix control. Physical Review X, 12(3). https://doi.org/10.1103/physrevx.12.031028

citation.article_title: Ligand field design enables quantum manipulation of spins in Ni2+ complexes
citation.author: Wojnar
citation.unstructured_citation: Wojnar, M. K., Kundu, K., Kairalapova, A., Wang, X., Ozarowski, A., Berkelbach, T. C., Hill, S., & Freedman, D. E. (2024). Ligand field design enables quantum manipulation of spins in Ni2+ complexes. Chem. Sci., 15, 1374–1383. https://doi.org/10.1039/D3SC04919A

citation.article_title: Laser-induced ultrafast demagnetization: Femtomagnetism, a new frontier?
citation.author: Zhang
citation.journal_title: Spin dynamics in confined magnetic structures i
citation.unstructured_citation: Zhang, G., Hübner, W., Beaurepaire, E., & Bigot, J.-Y. (2002). Laser-induced ultrafast demagnetization: Femtomagnetism, a new frontier? In B. Hillebrands & K. Ounadjela (Eds.), Spin dynamics in confined magnetic structures i (pp. 245–289). Springer Berlin Heidelberg. https://doi.org/10.1007/3-540-40907-6_8

citation.article_title: Could the quantum internet be comprised of molecular spins with tunable optical interfaces?
citation.author: Laorenza
citation.journal_title: Journal of the American Chemical Society
citation.unstructured_citation: Laorenza, D. W., & Freedman, D. E. (2022). Could the quantum internet be comprised of molecular spins with tunable optical interfaces? Journal of the American Chemical Society, 144(48), 21810–21825. https://doi.org/10.1021/jacs.2c07775

citation.article_title: Potential for spin-based information processing in a thin-film molecular semiconductor
citation.author: Warner
citation.unstructured_citation: Warner, M., Din, S., Tupitsyn, I. S., Morley, G. W., Stoneham, A. M., Gardener, J. A., Wu, Z., Fisher, A. J., Heutz, S., Kay, C. W. M., & Aeppli, G. (2013). Potential for spin-based information processing in a thin-film molecular semiconductor. Nature, 503(7477), 504–508. https://doi.org/10.1038/nature12597

citation.author: Laorenza

citation.journal_title: The Journal of Physical Chemistry A

The Journal of Physical Chemistry A, 126(43), 8007–8020. https://doi.org/10.1021/acs.jpca.2c06854
